title: Senecio corrugatus
intro: Senecio corrugatus, commonly known as the corrugated ragwort or corrugated Jacob's ladder, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Senecio corrugatus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Senecio corrugatus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to Australia and belongs to the genus Senecio, which comprises over 1,000 species of plants. The genus Senecio is further classified into several sections, with Senecio corrugatus being part of the section Senecio. The species is also known to be a member of the tribe Senecioneae within the Asteraceae family. The classification of Senecio corrugatus reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Asteraceae family.
